mnemonics: HAVING Your Cake After GROUPing: You can't use HAVING to filter aggregate results until you've first used GROUP BY to create the groups.
keyPoints: GROUP BY is used to group rows sharing a property into summary rows.
HAVING is used to filter groups based on a condition, unlike WHERE which filters individual rows.
Columns in the SELECT clause must either be in the GROUP BY clause or be aggregate functions (like COUNT, SUM).
commonMistakes: Using WHERE instead of HAVING to filter on aggregate functions (e.g., SUM(salary) > 50000).
Including a non-aggregated column in the SELECT list that isn't part of the GROUP BY clause.
